catalogue_metadata.scope_notes: Patterns extracted only from the last merge to main, PR #132 (merge commit 8ee130099318722540692c3a80a3e9e628995084), focusing on newly introduced or materially reshaped agentic control, state, contract, and persistence behavior.
analysis_limits: Scoped to the merged delta from 6b2b61a..8ee1300 rather than the entire repository history.
Patterns are limited to evidence visible in code and tests touched by PR #132.
UI styling and general Gradio layout changes were ignored unless they changed agentic contracts or orchestration behavior.

system_map.overview: PR #132 consolidates the system around a layered intelligence pipeline: persisted weekly snapshots remain the durable record, derived intelligence snapshots become the UI-facing state object, and the orchestrator rebuilds or reuses week-scoped intelligence depending on cache and dirtiness checks. Visualization moves from transient live figures toward persisted chart artefacts that can be serialized, reloaded, and rendered independently of the original orchestration run.

agents.name: RunnerOrchestrator
agents.role: Central coordinator that runs the deterministic base pipeline, selects affected weeks, decides whether intelligence should be reused or rebuilt, and emits the DTO/UI contract.

agents.name: InsightsAgent
agents.role: Generates structured coaching insights for week-scoped intelligence contexts.

agents.name: PlanAgent
agents.role: Produces structured weekly plan content that is attached to the intelligence snapshot and DTO contract.

agents.name: VisualizationAgent
agents.role: Generates chart specifications that are rendered and then serialized into persisted image artefacts.

agents.name: SnapshotService
agents.role: Coordinates recompute, week-scoped agent context reconstruction, cache invalidation, dirtiness checks, and persistence of weekly and chart artefacts.

orchestration.description: A central orchestrator runs a sequential deterministic pipeline first, then performs a per-week intelligence loop with selective reuse, cache-aside rebuilds, and optional parallel execution of intelligence and visualization steps.

tools_and_integrations.name: LLM client layer
tools_and_integrations.purpose: Provider abstraction and structured-output transport for decision-making agents.

tools_and_integrations.name: WeeklySnapshotRepository
tools_and_integrations.purpose: Stores persisted weekly truth and accepts later chart enrichment updates.

tools_and_integrations.name: Chart serialization helpers
tools_and_integrations.purpose: Converts figures and images into base64 strings for persistence and decodes them back into UI-safe image objects.

state_and_memory.description: Weekly snapshots persist aggregate metrics plus serialized chart artefacts in SQLite.
Intelligence snapshots are cached in memory by (week_start, language) and reused for lazy week selection.
RunnerIntelligenceSnapshot projects persisted and computed state into a UI-facing aggregate without becoming the persistence source of truth.

patterns.name: Intelligence Snapshot Projection Boundary

tool_protocols.notes: Agents produce structured outputs and chart specs, while the snapshot builder aggregates the resulting shared context into a UI-facing model.

optimisation_targets.notes: The projection boundary reduces direct UI dependence on orchestration internals.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: Agent outputs remain autonomous, but the final UI contract is system-shaped through a deterministic projection layer.

patterns.summary: The merge introduces a distinct derived model that aggregates agent and service outputs into a stable UI-facing intelligence snapshot.
patterns.intent: Separate persisted training truth from the richer state needed to drive UI and coaching interactions.
patterns.context: This appears in the refactor that positions WeeklySnapshot as persisted truth and RunnerIntelligenceSnapshot as the UI-facing derivative.
forces: UI needs richer state than persisted weekly aggregates alone provide.
Persisted storage should remain simpler than the full runtime intelligence model.
Orchestrator internals were previously leaking into UI rendering.
patterns.solution: A dedicated builder projects PipelineContext into RunnerIntelligenceSnapshot using safe extraction from summary, positioning, recommendation, trend, goal, and chart state, then the snapshot is serialized separately for DTO delivery.
implementation_signals: New `build_intelligence_snapshot(...)` function replaces direct UI dependence on raw orchestrator state.
New `RunnerAPIResponse.intelligence_snapshot` field carries the serialized projection.

evidence.note: Builds a UI-facing snapshot from already-computed context rather than recomputing business logic.
Introduces intelligence_snapshot as an explicit product surface in the DTO.
Builds and attaches intelligence_snapshot before returning the response contract.

benefits: Makes the UI consume a stable boundary object instead of ad hoc orchestrator fields.
Allows week-scoped intelligence to be rebuilt or cached without changing the UI contract.
costs: Adds another representation layer to maintain.
Requires careful synchronization between projected fields and underlying context changes.
failure_modes: Projected fields can drift from source context if builder logic is incomplete.
If the serializer omits fields, the UI may still need direct fallbacks.

patterns.reuse_notes: Use this when agent/service pipelines produce many intermediate fields but the consumer should depend on one stable aggregate view.

patterns.name: DTO-Backed UI Contract

tool_protocols.notes: The pattern is about the orchestrator-to-UI boundary rather than inter-agent protocols.

optimisation_targets.notes: DTOs reduce UI dependence on internal orchestrator structure.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: The UI reads a constrained response object instead of deep orchestration state.

patterns.summary: The merge formalizes a response DTO so the UI reads a controlled output contract rather than arbitrary orchestration state.
patterns.intent: Reduce implicit coupling between orchestration internals and UI rendering logic.
patterns.context: The orchestrator now returns a structured RunnerAPIResponse with intelligence_snapshot included as a first-class field.
forces: UI code previously depended on loosely shaped dictionaries and internal state.
Multiple execution paths need a common, serializable response surface.
patterns.solution: Define a dataclass DTO, populate it centrally in the orchestrator, and update the UI helpers to use attribute access instead of reconstructing response shape locally.
implementation_signals: New `RunnerAPIResponse` dataclass in application/dto.
UI code shifts from dict-style `.get(...)` assumptions to orchestrator-returned response attributes.

evidence.note: Defines the response contract returned to the UI.
Constructs RunnerAPIResponse at the end of orchestration.
Reads the response object directly instead of expecting a free-form dict.

benefits: Creates one explicit backend-to-UI contract.
Makes serialization failures easier to isolate.
costs: Adds an extra mapping layer in the orchestrator.
Requires DTO updates whenever product surface changes.
failure_modes: DTO fields can become a grab bag if not curated.
Fallback DTO generation may mask root-cause serialization issues.

patterns.reuse_notes: Useful when a single orchestrator serves a UI that is evolving faster than the internal pipeline structure.

patterns.name: Persisted Chart Artifact Pipeline

tool_protocols.notes: VisualizationAgent output is rendered and then transformed into persisted image artefacts.

optimisation_targets.notes: Persisted artefacts make UI rendering independent of the original live figure object.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: Charts remain dynamically generated, but the runtime contract stabilizes around serialized artefacts.

patterns.summary: The merge converts visualization outputs into serialized image artefacts that can be persisted, reloaded, and rendered without live figure state.
patterns.intent: Break the dependency between UI rendering and in-memory Matplotlib figure lifecycle.
patterns.context: This appears in the visualization and persistence refactor that standardizes chart handling as dict-based image artefacts.
forces: Figures are not naturally JSON-serializable.
Historical weeks and restarts need reproducible chart rendering.
UI should consume persisted artefacts the same way for live and historical paths.
patterns.solution: Convert charts to base64 strings for storage, store them alongside weekly snapshots, decode them back into PIL images for the UI, and serialize charts again when the intelligence snapshot is returned.
implementation_signals: New helper functions `figure_to_base64`, `serialize_charts`, and `decode_chart`.
Repository-level `save_intelligence_snapshot(...)` persists charts by snapshot id.

evidence.note: Implements the artifact conversion pipeline between figure, base64, and UI image.
Persists serialized charts via snapshot id rather than reusing transient UI state.
Re-serializes snapshot charts when sending the DTO/UI contract.

benefits: Charts survive restarts and historical reconstruction paths.
The UI can render persisted charts consistently.
costs: Adds image encoding and decoding overhead.
Introduces another serialization format boundary to maintain.
failure_modes: Corrupt base64 or unsupported objects can degrade chart rendering.
Partial updates can leave weekly metrics and chart artefacts out of sync.

patterns.reuse_notes: Use when a visualization-producing agent or tool returns non-serializable runtime objects that must survive persistence and replay.

patterns.name: Snapshot ID-Based Partial Enrichment

tool_protocols.notes: This is a persistence coordination pattern within the orchestrator and snapshot service.

optimisation_targets.notes: Preserving stable identity avoids writing chart enrichments onto the wrong weekly record.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: Identity preservation constrains enrichment to one snapshot instance rather than regenerating independent records.

patterns.summary: The merge preserves weekly snapshot identity so later intelligence and chart enrichment can target a stable persisted record.
patterns.intent: Avoid losing or overwriting chart attachments when a week is recomputed and then enriched later.
patterns.context: The service preserves an existing snapshot id during recompute and later uses that id for chart-specific updates.
forces: Weekly metrics are recomputed before intelligence artefacts are added.
Language and week-based updates can otherwise target the wrong record.
patterns.solution: When recomputing a week, reuse the existing cached snapshot id if present; then update intelligence-specific fields such as charts by snapshot id rather than only by week key.
implementation_signals: Recompute path assigns `weekly_snapshot.id` from the affected cached snapshot.
Repository update for charts uses `WHERE id = ?`.

evidence.note: Preserves snapshot id from an existing affected snapshot during recompute.
Performs chart enrichment by snapshot id rather than by week_start_date.

benefits: Preserves chart associations across recomputes.
Supports later partial enrichment of already-persisted weekly rows.
costs: Makes the persistence lifecycle multi-phase.
Requires careful id propagation between cache, service, and repository.
failure_modes: Missing or stale ids can still orphan enrichments.
If the cache is wrong, the wrong snapshot id may be preserved.

patterns.reuse_notes: Useful when derived artefacts are added after the main persistence write and must target a stable aggregate record.

patterns.name: Week-Scoped Repository Reconstruction

tool_protocols.notes: The orchestrator reconstructs per-week context from repositories before running intelligence agents.

optimisation_targets.notes: Repository-backed reconstruction ensures intelligence uses the full week state, not just the latest upload slice.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: Agents still decide over the context, but the system now reconstructs that context deterministically from persisted history.

patterns.summary: The merge reconstructs intelligence and visualization contexts from repository-backed week state instead of relying only on newly uploaded runs.
patterns.intent: Make historical weeks behave the same as current weeks when intelligence or charts are requested.
patterns.context: The orchestrator now fetches all runs for each affected week from the repository and rebuilds summary/trend context for that week before invoking intelligence steps.
forces: Upload batches may contain only a subset of runs relevant to a week.
Historical week navigation must not depend on the original upload session state.
patterns.solution: Use run repository lookups and week-specific summary/trend reconstruction helpers to prepare each week context before intelligence and visualization execution.
implementation_signals: Orchestrator fetches `all_week_runs` from `run_repo.get_runs_for_week(...)` before per-week intelligence.
SnapshotService adds `build_agent_context_for_week(target_date)`.

evidence.note: Both upload-time and on-demand intelligence paths reconstruct week-specific state from repositories.
Builds summary and trends centered on a specific week using persisted weekly history.

benefits: Improves consistency between current and historical week behavior.
Makes chart and insight generation less sensitive to upload path shape.
costs: Increases repository reads during orchestration.
Adds more context-building logic to the service layer.
failure_modes: Repository gaps can still force fallback to session runs.
If persisted runner identity is wrong, the wrong week data may be reconstructed.

patterns.reuse_notes: Use when agent execution should depend on durable period state rather than only the latest request payload.

patterns.name: Language-Keyed Intelligence Cache

tool_protocols.notes: Cache control is an orchestrator/service memory pattern rather than an external tool protocol.

optimisation_targets.notes: The cache prevents unnecessary rebuilds while avoiding stale language reuse.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: Caching is simple operationally, but keyed by language to respect multilingual agent outputs.

patterns.summary: The merge uses `(week_start, language)` as the intelligence cache key so multilingual outputs are reused safely.
patterns.intent: Avoid stale cross-language reuse when intelligence is rebuilt lazily or week selection changes.
patterns.context: Cached intelligence snapshots are stored and retrieved by week plus language, and cache misses trigger rebuild instead of language fallback reuse.
forces: Intelligence and UI text are language-specific.
Week selection should be fast, but cached content must remain semantically valid.
patterns.solution: Key intelligence snapshots by both week and language, clear matching keys during invalidation, and return `None` when a week exists only in another language so the orchestrator rebuilds it.
implementation_signals: SnapshotService cache uses `Dict[Tuple[date, str], Any]`.
Orchestrator returns `None` if only another-language snapshot exists for the week.

evidence.note: Defines and manages language-keyed snapshot cache entries.
Prevents reusing a week snapshot from the wrong language.

benefits: Prevents stale localized UI content.
Supports cheap repeated week views in the same language.
costs: Increases cache footprint.
Makes cache invalidation slightly more complex.
failure_modes: Language-specific cache misses can increase rebuild frequency.
Incomplete invalidation can leave stale entries for some languages.

patterns.reuse_notes: Use when agent outputs are localized and cache reuse must remain language-safe.

patterns.name: Dirty-Check Gated Lazy Recompute

tool_protocols.notes: The orchestrator gates expensive agent execution on deterministic week state comparisons.

optimisation_targets.notes: Avoids rerunning LLM-backed intelligence when stable training metrics have not changed.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: A deterministic gate reduces unnecessary autonomy without removing lazy rebuild behavior.

patterns.summary: The merge adds a deterministic dirtiness guard so week intelligence is recomputed only when stable training signals change or on-demand rebuild is forced.
patterns.intent: Reduce unnecessary LLM work while keeping week views correct when data actually changes.
patterns.context: The orchestrator tests dirtiness before reusing or rebuilding cached week snapshots and before skipping historical intelligence.
forces: Weekly navigation can trigger repeated requests for the same data.
LLM-based recomputation is more expensive than deterministic cache checks.
patterns.solution: Compare only stable summary fields such as weekly distance, run count, pace, and consistency against the cached intelligence snapshot; rebuild only when they differ beyond tolerance or when mode is on-demand.
implementation_signals: `SnapshotService.is_week_dirty(...)` compares projected snapshot fields against WeeklySummary.
Orchestrator branches into reuse or recompute based on `is_dirty` and `mode`.

evidence.note: Defines the deterministic gating logic for reuse vs recompute.
Uses dirty-check results to reuse, skip, or recompute week intelligence.
Verifies historical weeks are skipped initially and rebuilt on demand.

benefits: Reduces repeated agent invocations.
Makes lazy week navigation faster and cheaper.
costs: Requires maintenance of the stable-field comparison set.
Can miss semantic changes not captured by the dirty metrics.
failure_modes: False negatives can preserve stale intelligence.
False positives can cause unnecessary recomputation.

patterns.reuse_notes: Use when expensive agent outputs are downstream of a smaller stable metric set that can be compared deterministically.

patterns.name: Active-Week Selective Intelligence

tool_protocols.notes: The heuristic limits where LLM-backed intelligence runs and where deterministic fallback is used.

optimisation_targets.notes: Historical weeks are degraded to deterministic behavior unless explicitly requested.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: The system constrains autonomous generation to active or on-demand weeks.

patterns.summary: The merge limits full intelligence generation to active or explicitly requested weeks and uses deterministic fallback for non-active historical weeks.
patterns.intent: Concentrate expensive intelligence generation on the weeks most likely to matter in the UI while preserving readable historical output.
patterns.context: During multi-week orchestration, only active or on-demand weeks run full intelligence, while non-active historical weeks can receive deterministic brief generation.
forces: Historical weeks still need a usable view.
Running full LLM-backed intelligence for all weeks is costly and slow.
patterns.solution: Select active weeks, set `enable_intelligence` accordingly for each week context, and in snapshot recompute choose between LLM brief generation and deterministic brief fallback based on `enable_intelligence` and comparison availability.
implementation_signals: Orchestrator computes `active_weeks` before the per-week loop.
SnapshotService chooses deterministic brief generation when intelligence is disabled for a week.

evidence.note: Selects active weeks and toggles `enable_intelligence` per week.
Uses deterministic brief fallback when `enable_intelligence` is false for historical weeks.

benefits: Keeps current-week experience richer than bulk historical processing.
Reduces cost and response time for multi-week orchestration.
costs: Historical weeks may present less rich intelligence unless explicitly requested.
Active-week selection becomes a critical product heuristic.
failure_modes: Wrong active-week selection can hide intelligence where users expect it.
Fallback briefs may feel inconsistent relative to LLM-generated weeks.

patterns.reuse_notes: Useful when only a subset of periods needs full generative analysis and the remainder can degrade gracefully.

patterns.name: Cache-Aside Week Intelligence Builder

tool_protocols.notes: The orchestrator lazily invokes intelligence steps only on cache miss or incomplete snapshot.

optimisation_targets.notes: Defers expensive intelligence work until a week is actually requested.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: The build is autonomous once triggered, but only triggered when the cache contract demands it.

patterns.summary: The merge introduces a cache-aside path for week intelligence so dormant weeks are materialized only when the UI requests them.
patterns.intent: Support interactive week selection without precomputing full intelligence for every historical period.
patterns.context: The UI can ask for a specific week, and the orchestrator returns a cached intelligence snapshot if complete or builds it on demand from persisted data.
forces: Historical intelligence can be expensive to precompute.
Week-level UI navigation must remain responsive.
patterns.solution: Implement `get_or_build_intelligence_snapshot(...)` as a cache-aside function that checks completeness first, reconstructs week context from repositories on miss, runs only intelligence-related steps, stores the result, and returns it.
implementation_signals: New `get_or_build_intelligence_snapshot(...)` path in orchestrator.
Tests verify the same week is not rebuilt twice.

evidence.note: Implements lazy rebuild from persisted weekly context and caches the result.
Verifies on-demand build and subsequent cache hit for the same week.

benefits: Avoids eager computation for rarely viewed weeks.
Reuses one orchestrator pathway for both precomputed and on-demand intelligence.
costs: Adds another execution path that must stay consistent with the main run path.
Requires a completeness check to avoid returning partial snapshots.
failure_modes: Incomplete snapshot detection can be wrong.
Repository reads can fail after the original upload session is gone.

patterns.reuse_notes: Apply when an agentic system supports interactive drill-down into periods or entities that should not all be materialized eagerly.

patterns.name: Scope-Aware Visualization Context

tool_protocols.notes: Visualization consumes a `visualization_scope` plus week runs/full runs split supplied by the orchestrator.

optimisation_targets.notes: Separating weekly and full-scope views fixes wrong-chart generation across historical and aggregated paths.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: The agent remains free to choose chart specs, but the orchestration layer defines the data scope explicitly.

patterns.summary: The merge adds explicit visualization scope so agents and rendering logic know whether they are producing week-scoped or full-run charts.
patterns.intent: Prevent confusion between week-local charts and charts aggregated across all affected weeks.
patterns.context: Per-week intelligence uses `week_runs` and `visualization_scope='weekly'`, while top-level results can later run a full-scope chart generation pass.
forces: The same visualization machinery serves both week detail and aggregate upload results.
Historical and current week charts were previously affected by scope confusion.
patterns.solution: Carry visualization scope in the pipeline context, populate week-specific runs before week intelligence, and optionally run a later full-scope chart pass for the top-level response when multiple weeks are affected.
implementation_signals: PipelineContext includes `visualization_scope` and `week_runs`.
Orchestrator sets weekly scope during per-week processing and `full` for top-level chart generation.

evidence.note: Carries visualization scope and separate week/full run collections.
Uses weekly scope inside the per-week loop and full scope for final aggregate charts.
Consumes the context shaped by orchestrator scope decisions.

benefits: Improves chart correctness for historical and aggregate flows.
Makes scope handling explicit rather than inferred from whatever runs happen to be present.
costs: Adds more context fields and branch logic.
Scope bugs can still occur if callers set the wrong mode.
failure_modes: Charts can still be wrong if week_runs and full runs are mixed.
Consumers may misread top-level charts as week-scoped if labels are weak.

patterns.reuse_notes: Use when the same agent or tool generates artefacts for both local and aggregated scopes and needs an explicit control flag.

patterns.name: Deterministic Brief Degradation

tool_protocols.notes: Brief generation falls back to deterministic text when intelligence should not or cannot run.

optimisation_targets.notes: The system avoids empty brief state and avoids unnecessary LLM work when comparison or active-week conditions are not met.

simplicity_vs_autonomy.rationale: Generation is explicitly degraded to deterministic output under constrained conditions.

patterns.summary: The merge introduces deterministic brief fallback when there is insufficient comparison context or intelligence is intentionally disabled for the week.
patterns.intent: Ensure week summaries remain present and predictable even when generative coaching should not run.
patterns.context: Snapshot recompute uses the brief service only when comparison exists and intelligence is enabled; otherwise it writes deterministic boilerplate suitable for onboarding or historical weeks.
forces: Historical or first-week periods may not have enough evidence for useful LLM comparison.
The UI still expects a readable brief field.
patterns.solution: Branch brief generation on comparison availability and intelligence enablement, generating deterministic fallback text for no-data, first-week, or non-active-week cases.
implementation_signals: `reason` values differentiate active-week LLM generation from fallback paths.
A dedicated `_generate_deterministic_brief(...)` method supplies default text.

evidence.note: Implements conditional brief generation with deterministic degradation paths.

benefits: Prevents empty or unstable brief fields.
Improves robustness for historical or low-data weeks.
costs: Fallback text can feel generic.
Multiple brief-generation paths must stay aligned with UI expectations.
failure_modes: Overuse of fallback can make the product feel less personalized.
Incorrect gating can suppress LLM generation when it should run.

patterns.reuse_notes: Use when a generative summary should degrade to predictable text in low-evidence or cost-constrained contexts.

anti_patterns.name: Non-Atomic Intelligence Enrichment
symptoms: Weekly snapshot persistence and later chart enrichment happen in separate phases.
Intelligence snapshot data is only partly durable, with charts persisted but most intelligence fields kept in memory.

where_observed.note: Shows separate phases for recompute and later intelligence enrichment.
Charts are updated after the main weekly snapshot write.
Stores intelligence snapshots in memory and only persists chart artefacts.

impact: A restart can preserve charts but lose other intelligence state.
A failure between phases can leave persisted weekly data and intelligence artefacts out of sync.
suggested_remediation: Define whether intelligence snapshot is meant to become a fully durable aggregate or remain explicitly ephemeral.
If durability is desired, persist a complete serialized intelligence snapshot or introduce transactional enrichment.

anti_patterns.name: Runner Identity Placeholder Leak
symptoms: Repository reads return `config.DEFAULT_RUNNER_ID` instead of the row's runner_id.
Week reconstruction logic implicitly assumes one runner identity.

where_observed.note: Reads persisted weekly snapshots back with a default placeholder runner id.

impact: Multi-runner semantics are weaker than the schema suggests.
Repository-backed week reconstruction can read logically incorrect ownership metadata.
suggested_remediation: Read and validate `row["runner_id"]` in the repository model mapper.
Add repository-level tests covering more than the default runner.

cross_cutting_concerns.approach: The merge strengthens output shaping indirectly by introducing deterministic projection and DTO boundaries after agent execution, reducing the number of UI consumers that depend on raw agent payloads.
cross_cutting_concerns.notes: The contract shift is structural rather than prompt-text heavy in this merge.

cross_cutting_concerns.approach: Historical and low-context weeks degrade to deterministic brief output instead of forcing LLM generation without comparison evidence.
cross_cutting_concerns.notes: This is a quality and operational safety measure, not a clinical safety rule.

cross_cutting_concerns.approach: Week dirtiness checks, active-week selection, and cache-aside rebuilding reduce unnecessary intelligence recomputation.
cross_cutting_concerns.notes: The merge treats lazy intelligence as a first-class orchestration optimization.

cross_cutting_concerns.approach: New cache-hit, recompute, and brief-generation branches emit structured events and trace-aware logs through the orchestrator and snapshot service.
cross_cutting_concerns.notes: Operational visibility supports debugging the now more complex lazy and multi-phase paths.

open_questions.question: Should RunnerIntelligenceSnapshot become fully persisted or remain intentionally ephemeral apart from charts?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The merge stabilizes the UI contract around this model, but persistence still treats it as partly cache-only state.
open_questions.evidence_gap: No full repository or schema for intelligence snapshots exists in the merged code.
open_questions.suggested_next_check: Inspect roadmap items and later commits for intelligence snapshot persistence decisions.

open_questions.question: Is active-week selection a product rule or only an optimization heuristic?
open_questions.why_it_matters: It materially changes which weeks receive full generative intelligence versus fallback text.
open_questions.evidence_gap: The merge implements the behavior but does not document the rule as a stable product contract.
open_questions.suggested_next_check: Inspect the `_select_active_weeks(...)` policy and product-facing docs/tests that justify it.

open_questions.question: How much of the UI is now a pure consumer of intelligence_snapshot versus still reconstructing presentation logic locally?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The merge direction is toward a cleaner boundary, but comments and helper code suggest the transition is incomplete.
open_questions.evidence_gap: The UI still contains shaping helpers and fallback formatting logic.
open_questions.suggested_next_check: Compare gradio_app consumers against fields already present in RunnerIntelligenceSnapshot.
